guest_credentials: Former professional cyclist who raced for one of the biggest teams in the world; now a coach and mentor helping others dismantle emotional blocks and self-imposed limitations
key_claims: Emotional blockages prevent access to limitless potential by suppressing emotions that have been conditioned out of expression since childhood
Most limitations are learned, not innate, and can be unlearned through introspection and emotional processing
Small daily acts aligned with personal values, compounded over time, create transformation described as 'first slowly then suddenly'
Triggers in daily life are surface symptoms; the real work is investigating what beliefs lie beneath the trigger
There is never a point of no return for transformative change, regardless of how long someone has lived a misaligned life
cluster_reasoning: The episode centers on mental health, emotional processing, and dismantling self-imposed limitations—core recovery and longevity themes—while Shannon's background as a former pro adds community/culture context.
